paper_title: Cadmium and Thiol Dynamics in Diatoms

methods.description: Procedure for maintaining the stock culture of Thalassiosira weissflogii and preparing media and labware for experiments.

steps.description: The stock of Thalassiosira weissflogii (CCMP 1587), obtained from Provasoli-Guillard National Center for Marine Algae and Microbiota, Bigelow Laboratory, was maintained in f/2 medium 17 in a growth incubator (23 ± 1 °C, 120 μ mol photons m −2 s −1 , under a 14-h light:10-h dark regimen).
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain diatom stock culture under specified temperature, light intensity, and light-dark cycle conditions.
inputs: Light source (120 μmol photons m−2 s−1)

steps.description: Throughout this study, 0.22 μ m-filtered and chelexed (with Chelex 100 resin, Bio-Rad Laboratories, CA) Aquil artificial seawater (containing NaCl, KBr, KCl, H 3 BO 3 , Na 2 SO 4 , NaHCO 3 , NaF, CaCl 2 , SrCl 2 and MgCl 2 , and without EDTA, trace metals or vitamins) 18 was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare Aquil artificial seawater by filtering and chelexing to remove trace metals.
inputs: Aquil artificial seawater components (NaCl, KBr, KCl, H3BO3, Na2SO4, NaHCO3, NaF, CaCl2, SrCl2, MgCl2)

steps.description: Stocks for the f/2 medium (N, P, Si and vitamins) were also passed through the column with Chelex 100 ion-exchange resin to remove any background metals.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare f/2 medium stocks by passing through Chelex resin.
inputs: f/2 medium stock components (N, P, Si, vitamins)

steps.description: The pH value of the medium was maintained at 8.2 ± 0.1.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain the pH of the culture medium at 8.2 ± 0.1.

steps.description: All the polycarbonate flasks and bottles were soaked in 5% HCl for at least 24 h and rinsed seven times with Milli-Q water (18.2 MΩ) before the experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean polycarbonate flasks and bottles by soaking in HCl and rinsing with Milli-Q water.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring the net uptake rate of cadmium into Thalassiosira weissflogii cells under different irradiance levels and Cd concentrations.

steps.description: T. weissflogii cells were first transferred from the f/2 culture medium to f/2-f/20 medium (with f/20 levels of trace metals without EDTA or the addition of Cu and Zn).
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer diatom cells from f/2 medium to f/2-f/20 medium.

steps.description: and acclimated at three levels of irradiance (60, 120 and 240 μ mol photons m −2 s −1 ) for two weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: Acclimate diatom cells at different irradiance levels for two weeks.
inputs: Irradiance levels (60, 120, 240 μmol photons m−2 s−1)

steps.description: During the acclimation period, the cell density was measured daily with a hemocytometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure cell density daily using a hemocytometer.

steps.description: and the culture medium was renewed every 3-5 days to ensure exponential growth of the cells.
steps.searchable_summary: Renew culture medium every 3-5 days.

steps.description: Afterward, a 4-h uptake experiment was conducted on the irradiance-conditioned cells. Mid-log growing diatoms were harvested by centrifugation (3000 rpm, 23 °C, 10 min).
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest mid-log growing diatoms by centrifugation.

steps.description: and resuspended in 250 mL of pre-equilibrated Cd uptake medium.
steps.searchable_summary: Resuspend harvested cells in pre-equilibrated Cd uptake medium.
outputs: Cells in Cd uptake experiment setup

steps.description: Seawater not enriched with nutrients was employed for the uptake experiments. There were four Cd-concentration treatments with two replicates each in every irradiance test, and the total dissolved Cd concentrations (added as a certified Cd ICP standard solution, 1000 mg L −1 , NSI solutions) were 2.7 × 10 −8 , 8.9 × 10 −8 , 2.7 × 10 −7 , and 4.5 × 10 −7 mol L −1 . Nitrilotriacetate (NTA, 0.1 mM) was added to maintain a constant free Cd ion concentration ([Cd 2+ ]). After the addition of ultrapure NaOH (1 M) to adjust the pH (8.2 ± 0.1), the uptake medium was equilibrated overnight before use.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare Cd uptake medium with specific total Cd concentrations, NTA, adjusted pH, and equilibrate overnight.
inputs: Certified Cd ICP standard solution (1000 mg L−1)
Desired total Cd concentrations (2.7e-8, 8.9e-8, 2.7e-7, 4.5e-7 mol L−1)
outputs: Pre-equilibrated Cd uptake medium at different Cd concentrations

steps.description: The calculated [Cd 2+ ] (obtained using the Visual MINTEQ software, version 3.1) for each corresponding treatment was 5.49 × 10 −10 , 1.83 × 10 −9 , 5.50 × 10 −9 , and 9.17 × 10 −9 mol L −1 , respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate free Cd ion concentration using Visual MINTEQ software.
inputs: Cd uptake medium composition (total Cd, NTA, pH, etc.)

steps.description: The uptake of Cd into the diatoms was studied under three irradiance levels as described for the acclimation period.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct the 4-hour Cd uptake experiment under three irradiance levels.
inputs: Cells in Cd uptake medium (from Step 6)
Irradiance levels (60, 120, 240 μmol photons m−2 s−1)
outputs: Diatom cultures exposed to Cd for varying durations

steps.description: At 1, 2, 3 and 4 h of exposure, the intracellular Cd concentration was measured in each treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: Take samples from each replicate at 1, 2, 3, and 4 hours of exposure.
outputs: Sample aliquots at different time points

steps.description: For this measurement, a 20-mL sample from each replicate bottle was gently filtered (< 50 mmHg) through a 3-μ m polycarbonate membrane.
steps.searchable_summary: Gently filter 20-mL sample through a 3-μm polycarbonate membrane.

steps.description: rinsed three times with artificial seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse filtered cells three times with artificial seawater.

steps.description: and then resuspended in 10 mL of EDTA (0.1 mM) to remove the weakly surface-adsorbed Cd.
steps.searchable_summary: Resuspend rinsed cells in EDTA solution to remove surface-adsorbed Cd.

steps.description: The cells were collected again.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect cells again after EDTA treatment (e.g., by filtration or centrifugation).

steps.description: and oven-dried at 60 °C for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Oven-dry collected cells at 60 °C for 24 hours.

steps.description: and then digested in 1 mL of ultrapure concentrated nitric acid (Fisher Scientific, USA) overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest dried cells in ultrapure concentrated nitric acid overnight.

steps.description: followed by dilution with Milli-Q water.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute digested samples with Milli-Q water.

steps.description: The Cd samples were finally analyzed by inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS, Agilent 7700) with internal standards.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze Cd concentration in diluted samples using ICP-MS with internal standards.

steps.description: Additionally, a "control" treatment with irradiance acclimation but without Cd exposure was prepared to quantify Cd and thiols, and the Cd content of this treatment fell below the detection limit (0.02 ppb, 1.8 amol Cd cell −1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare a control treatment without Cd exposure and quantify background Cd content.
outputs: Background Cd content measurement (expected below detection limit)

steps.description: The number of cells at the beginning and end of the uptake experiments was counted with a microscope (at approximately 3-8 × 10 4 cells mL −1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Count cell number at the beginning and end of the uptake experiments using a microscope.
inputs: Culture samples (beginning and end of experiment)

steps.description: and all the concentrations in this study were expressed on a per cell basis.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate intracellular Cd concentration on a per cell basis.
outputs: Intracellular Cd concentration (e.g., amol Cd cell−1)

steps.description: and the (net) Cd uptake rate was calculated from the slope of the linear regression between the intracellular Cd concentrations and time.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the net Cd uptake rate using linear regression of intracellular Cd concentration versus time.
inputs: Intracellular Cd concentrations at different time points (from Step 21)
outputs: Net Cd uptake rate (e.g., amol Cd cell−1 h−1)

methods.description: Procedure for determining the levels of low molecular weight thiols (cysteine, γ-glutamylcysteine, GSH, PC2-4) in diatom cultures using HPLC with fluorescence detection.

steps.description: In tandem with the Cd uptake sampling, the diatom culture was also harvested for the determination of LMW thiols (cysteine, Cys; γ -glutamylcysteine, γ -EC; GSH; PC 2-4 ). A 200-mL aliquot was concentrated.
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest a 200-mL aliquot of diatom culture and concentrate it.

steps.description: rinsed three times with filtered artificial seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse concentrated cells three times with filtered artificial seawater.

steps.description: and then stored in 1 mL of an ice-cold 6.3 mM diethylenetriamine pentaacetic acid (DTPA) with 0.1% (v/v) trifluoroacetic acid (TFA) extraction solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Store rinsed cells in ice-cold DTPA/TFA extraction solution.
inputs: Ice-cold DTPA/TFA extraction solution (6.3 mM DTPA, 0.1% TFA, 1 mL)

steps.description: Afterward, algal cells were ultrasonically disrupted (6 times, 10 sec each time with 10 sec intervals), with a breakage efficiency of over 95%.
steps.searchable_summary: Ultrasonically disrupt algal cells.
inputs: Sonicator (6 cycles, 10 sec on, 10 sec off)

steps.description: The homogenate was then centrifuged at 13,000 g for 10 min (4 °C).
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge the homogenate at 13,000 g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.description: and the separated supernatant was subjected to pre-column derivatization as described in previous studies 20,21 .
steps.searchable_summary: Collect the supernatant for pre-column derivatization.

steps.description: Briefly, 250 μ L of supernatant was added to a disulfur reductant consisting of 615 μ L of 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-piperazine-1-pr opane-sulfonic acid (HEPPS, 200 mM in 6.3 mM DTPA, pH 8.2) and 25 μ L of tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine hydrochloride (TCEP, 20 mM in HEPPS buffer).
steps.searchable_summary: Add supernatant to a disulfur reductant mixture (HEPPS and TCEP).
inputs: HEPPS solution (200 mM in 6.3 mM DTPA, pH 8.2, 615 μL)
TCEP solution (20 mM in HEPPS buffer, 25 μL)

steps.description: A volume of 10 μ L of N-acetyl-L-cysteine (NAC, 0.5 mM) was also added as an internal standard to trace the HPLC performance.
steps.searchable_summary: Add N-acetyl-L-cysteine (NAC) as an internal standard.
outputs: Sample mixture with reductant and internal standard

steps.description: The mixture was placed in a 45 °C water bath for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate the mixture in a 45 °C water bath for 10 minutes.
inputs: Sample mixture with reductant and internal standard

steps.description: and later labeled with 10 μ L of the fluorescent dye monobromobimane (mBBr, 50 mM).
steps.searchable_summary: Label the mixture with the fluorescent dye monobromobimane (mBBr).

steps.description: After incubating at 45 °C in a water bath for another 30 min, these reactions were ceased by adding 100 μ L of methanesulfonic acid (MSA, 1 M).
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate the labeled mixture at 45 °C for 30 minutes, then stop the reaction by adding methanesulfonic acid (MSA).

steps.description: Simultaneously, a series of standard mixtures (with Cys, GSH and γ -EC at concentrations of 4, 8, 16, 32 and 40 pmol μ L −1 added and PC 2-4 at concentrations of 0.8, 1.6, 3.2, 6.4 and 8.0 pmol μ L −1 added) were treated in the same way to aid in quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and derivatize a series of thiol standard mixtures.
inputs: Thiol standards (Cys, GSH, γ-EC, PC2-4) at specified concentrations

steps.description: The levels of LMW thiols were finally determined by a gradient elution method 22 using an HPLC instrument (Thermo U-3000) equipped with a Phenomenex Synergi 4 μ Hydro-RP C 18 column (100 × 4.6 mm) and an online FLD-3100 fluorescence detector.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine LMW thiol levels using HPLC with a C18 column and fluorescence detector, using a gradient elution method.
inputs: Phenomenex Synergi 4 μ Hydro-RP C18 column (100 × 4.6 mm)

steps.description: The (net) induction rate (I, amol cell −1 h −1 ) of PC thiols was calculated as the time-weighted average of C according to the following equation 23 : where t i is the time of exposure (h), and C i is the concentration of the newly induced thiols after t i .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the net induction rate of PC thiols using the time-weighted average formula.
inputs: PC thiol concentrations at different time points (from Step 13)
outputs: Net PC thiol induction rate (amol cell−1 h−1)

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to evaluate differences among experimental treatments.

steps.description: Differences among treatments were evaluated using a one-way ANOVA (SPSS 16.0 software).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way ANOVA to evaluate differences among treatments.

steps.description: with Duncan's post hoc multiple comparisons test (SPSS 16.0 software).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Duncan's post hoc multiple comparisons test.

steps.description: The differences were considered significant when p < 0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine statistical significance based on a p-value threshold of 0.05.
inputs: p-values from ANOVA and post hoc tests
